scenario: Atlas Bank operates a Camunda 8.8 payments service that consumes 40000 transactions per day with a Spring Boot worker pool. The platform team reviews the camunda.client.worker.defaults block in application.yaml to confirm the out-of-the-box maximal interval between polls for new jobs before tuning the value for low-latency processing.
question: Which default value does the camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval property carry in Camunda 8.8?

options.text: Set to PT1S by default, defining the maximal interval between polls for new jobs on the worker
Set to PT0.1S by default, defining the maximal interval between polls for new jobs on the worker
Set to PT5M by default, defining the maximal interval between polls for new jobs on the worker
Set to PT30S by default, defining the maximal interval between polls for new jobs on the worker

a.text: Incorrect. PT1S is not the documented default; the reference table lists PT0.1S as the default poll interval on camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval.

b.text: Correct. The properties reference table lists PT0.1S as the default value for camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval; it bounds how long the worker waits between successive job-activation polls before issuing the next request.

c.text: Incorrect. PT5M is the documented default for camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout, the job ownership window; the poll-interval defaults to PT0.1S.

d.text: Incorrect. PT30S is not the documented default; the reference table lists PT0.1S as the default poll interval on camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval.

explanation: camunda.client.worker.defaults.poll-interval expresses the maximal duration between successive job-activation polls and defaults to PT0.1S. The related camunda.client.worker.defaults.timeout property defaults to PT5M and governs how long a job is exclusively assigned to one worker after activation.

docs.title: Camunda Spring Boot starter properties reference
